semanticCore: Throat, windpipe, larynx, vocal cords, or channel
quranicRelevance: Hearts reaching the throats in terror (Hanajir - 'balaghati al-quloobu al-hanajira', 'lada al-hanajiri').

keyDerivations.meaning: Throat, windpipe, larynx, vocal channel, or neck passage

figurativeOrRareUsages: إِذْ جَاءُوكُمْ مِنْ فَوْقِكُمْ وَمِنْ أَسْفَلَ مِنْكُمْ ... وَإِذْ زَاغَتِ الْأَبْصَارُ وَبَلَغَتِ الْقُلُوبُ الْحَنَاجِرَ (34:10): When they came upon you from above you and from below you... and when eyes shifted and hearts reached the throats (trench battle panic).
summaryEn: The root represents throats (Hanajir), serving as a physiological description of extreme battlefield panic where hearts feel as if they have risen to the throat.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر مجرى النفس في الحلق والممر الحنجري (حنجر)، مصوراً ذعر المؤمنين بغزوة الأحزاب بارتفاع ووصول نبض القلوب للحناجر خوفاً.
rawText:
حنجر
حَنْجرَةٌ : see art. حجر.
حَنْجَرَهُ , here mentioned in the K: see art. حجر, in which I have mentioned it as Q. Q., like the two words here following, which are mentioned in the latter art. in the S and K &c.
حُنْجُورٌ : see art. حجر.
